description_long: This is a large, ornately decorated ceramic vase with a wide, rounded body and a narrow pedestal base. The vase features two prominent, sculpted handles that curl upward in a spiral, reminiscent of ancient Greek or Roman kraters. The body is adorned with intricate painted scenes, likely depicting mythological or historical figures, rendered in earthy tones of terracotta, black, and faded gold. The rim is broad and slightly flared, with the handles attached just below it, adding to the vase's imposing presence. The back of the vase displays a more subdued floral or leaf motif, while the front is dominated by the detailed narrative artwork. The surface appears slightly matte with some signs of age and wear, suggesting it is an antique or a high-quality reproduction.
description: A large, antique-style ceramic vase with ornate spiral handles and detailed mythological scenes painted on its rounded body. The surface is matte and shows signs of age, with decorative motifs on both front and back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed painted scene and both ornate spiral handles prominently.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the floral or leaf motif and the backs of the thick, sculpted handles.
description_view_2: Angled front view with the mythological artwork visible and both handles in partial profile.
description_view_3: Angled rear view focusing on the decorative back pattern and the structure of the handles.
attribute_pattern: narrative figural scenes and floral motifs
